name: A-170
description: Dual slew limiter (portamento controller). Upper SL has single joint rise/fall time knob. Lower SL has independent rise and fall controls with 3-position time range switch for AR envelope use.

controls.name: Time
controls.description: Sets both rise and fall time jointly for the upper slew limiter.

controls.name: Rise (up arrow)
controls.description: Sets rise time for the lower slew limiter, within range set by Time Range switch.

controls.name: Fall (down arrow)
controls.description: Sets fall time for the lower slew limiter, within range set by Time Range switch.

controls.name: Time Range
controls.description: 3-position switch sets overall time range for lower SL rise and fall.
controls.detail: L = low (fast) · M = medium · H = high (slow)

inputs.name: In (Upper SL)
inputs.description: Signal input for the upper slew limiter.

inputs.name: In (Lower SL)
inputs.description: Signal input for the lower slew limiter.

outputs.name: Out (Upper SL)
outputs.description: Slew-limited output of the upper slew limiter.

outputs.name: Out (Lower SL)
outputs.description: Slew-limited output of the lower slew limiter.
outputs.voltage: follows input (note ~0.5V offset due to diodes)

behaviors.name: Portamento
behaviors.description: Smooths pitch CV for glide effect. Upper SL recommended for precision pitch work; lower SL has ~0.5V diode offset unsuitable for 1V/oct.

behaviors.name: AR Envelope Generator
behaviors.description: Lower SL with separate rise/fall controls acts as an AR envelope generator when a gate signal is applied.

behaviors.name: Complex Envelope Mixing
behaviors.description: Clock sequencer triggers multiple A-170 units whose envelope outputs are mixed in a linear mixer, creating complex evolving envelopes.

patchIdeas.name: Portamento generator
patchIdeas.patch: MIDI-CV → Upper SL In · Out → VCO 1V/oct · Time knob sets glide
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-170:
* Time (Upper SL): to taste
- MIDI-CV (CV) p> A-170 (In Upper SL)
- A-170 (Out Upper SL) p> VCO (1V/oct) // glide amount set by Time
steps: Patch your MIDI-CV or keyboard pitch CV into the Upper SL In jack.
Patch Upper SL Out into your VCO's 1V/oct input.
Start with the Time knob fully counter-clockwise (0 = no glide).
Play two notes far apart, then slowly turn Time clockwise until the pitch glides noticeably between them.
Use the Upper SL (not the lower one) for precision pitch — the lower SL has a ~0.5V diode offset that breaks 1V/oct tracking.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out (Upper SL) — smoothed pitch CV that lags behind the incoming notes by the Time setting.
patchIdeas.hear: Each note slides into the next instead of jumping. Short Time settings feel like a subtle legato; longer settings give a cartoonish glide.

patchIdeas.name: AR envelope for bass
patchIdeas.patch: Gate → Lower SL In · Rise/Fall set individually · Out → VCA CV
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-170:
* Time Range (Lower SL): M
- Gate Source (Gate) g> A-170 (In Lower SL)
- A-170 (Out Lower SL) >> VCA (CV) // AR shape from Rise/Fall knobs
steps: Flip the Time Range switch to M (medium, 0–10s).
Patch a gate from a sequencer or keyboard into the Lower SL In jack.
Patch Lower SL Out into your VCA's CV input.
Set the Rise (up arrow) knob low for a snappy attack.
Set the Fall (down arrow) knob around 9 o'clock for a short tail.
Play notes and tweak Rise/Fall until the envelope shape feels musical.
If the envelope is too slow or too fast overall, switch Time Range to L (faster) or H (slower).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out (Lower SL) — unipolar AR envelope following each incoming gate (starts from ~0.5V offset, not true 0V).
patchIdeas.hear: A classic attack-release bass shape. Short Rise gives a punchy pluck; longer Rise turns it into a swell. The Fall controls how long the note rings out.

patchIdeas.name: Glissando AM
patchIdeas.patch: Pitch CV → VCO1 direct & via Upper SL → VCO2 (modulator) · produces timbral sweep
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-170:
- Pitch CV (CV) p> VCO1 (1V/oct)
- Pitch CV (CV) p> A-170 (In Upper SL)
- A-170 (Out Upper SL) p> VCO2 (1V/oct) // lagged pitch for AM sweep
- VCO2 (Out) >> VCA1 (CV) // modulator controls VCA1
steps: Multiple your keyboard or sequencer pitch CV into two destinations.
Patch one copy straight to VCO1's 1V/oct input (this is the carrier).
Patch the other copy into the Upper SL In jack.
Patch Upper SL Out to VCO2's 1V/oct input (this is the modulator).
Tune VCO1 and VCO2 to a harmonically related interval (octave, fifth).
Patch VCO2's output into a VCA CV input; feed VCO1 through that VCA.
Set the Upper SL Time knob so the glide is shorter than your shortest note.
Play notes: VCO1 jumps instantly, VCO2 glides, producing a timbral sweep on each note.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out (Upper SL) — pitch CV that lags the played note, sent to VCO2 so the modulator catches up to the carrier during each note.
patchIdeas.hear: Every new note starts with a bright AM-style warble that settles into a clean tone as VCO2 arrives at pitch. Shorter Time = quick chirp; longer Time = longer sweep.

patchIdeas.name: Complex envelope from clock sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: A-161 outputs trigger Upper SL and Lower SL on one or more A-170s · envelopes mixed in A-138a · complex evolving shape
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-170:
* Time (Upper SL): slow
* Time Range (Lower SL): M

A-161:

A-138a:

- A-161 (Out 1) g> A-170 (In Upper SL)
- A-161 (Out 8) g> A-170 (In Lower SL)
- A-170 (Out Upper SL) >> A-138a (In 1)
- A-170 (Out Lower SL) >> A-138a (In 4)
- A-138a (Out) >> VCF (Cutoff)
steps: Feed an LFO or MIDI clock into the A-160 clock divider, which drives the A-161 sequencer.
Patch A-161 output 1 (gate) into the Upper SL In jack.
Patch A-161 output 8 (or any other) into the Lower SL In jack.
Set the Upper SL Time knob for a long, slow ramp.
Set Lower SL Time Range to M and dial in contrasting Rise/Fall times.
Patch both A-170 outputs into separate inputs of a linear mixer like the A-138a.
Patch the mixer output to a VCF cutoff or VCA CV.
Add more A-170s on other A-161 outputs to build richer stacked envelopes.
patchIdeas.signalOut: A-138a Out — summed stack of staggered AR/slew envelopes, one per A-161 output you used.
patchIdeas.hear: A continuously evolving CV landscape. Each sequencer step fires a different envelope, and overlapping tails at the mixer create shapes you cannot draw with a single envelope generator.
